_meta.note: Posterior μ + 95% CI on Cohen's d, random-effects Bayesian meta-analysis (DerSimonian-Laird τ + importance sampling). Effect sizes are persuasion → conversion outcomes; not all techniques have closed-loop revenue evidence — see Atlas for inclusion criteria.
_meta.interpretation: d ≈ 0.2 small, 0.5 medium, 0.8 large. CI excluding 0 = posterior P>0.95 the effect is positive.

techniques.name: Gain framing
techniques.definition: Emphasize positive outcomes of the desired behavior ("you'll save X") rather than negative consequences of not acting.
techniques.when_to_use: Customer is already curious / inbound. Activation moment.
techniques.when_to_avoid: Hard sell / contested negotiation — loss framing tends to outperform there.
techniques.example_skeleton: Just shipped X. Teams using it cut Y from Z to W in the first 30 days.

techniques.name: Loss framing
techniques.definition: Emphasize negative consequences of NOT adopting the behavior.
techniques.when_to_use: Buyer is already considering switching / dissatisfied with status quo.
techniques.when_to_avoid: First touch with a cold prospect — can read as fear-mongering.
techniques.example_skeleton: Most teams in your stage are burning $X/mo on Y. The fix takes 20 minutes.

techniques.name: Social proof
techniques.definition: Cite peers / similar entities who have already adopted.
techniques.when_to_use: Buyer is uncertain about category. Mid-funnel.
techniques.when_to_avoid: Buyer thinks of themselves as a category-of-one (rare founders, contrarians).
techniques.example_skeleton: 5 of the 10 fastest-growing X are already on Y.

techniques.name: Scarcity / urgency
techniques.definition: Limited availability, limited time, or limited slots.
techniques.when_to_use: Late-funnel — buyer has decided category, deciding vendor.
techniques.when_to_avoid: Cold first touch — false scarcity is the #1 trust-killer in B2B.
techniques.example_skeleton: We're taking on 3 design partners for Q3. 1 left.

techniques.name: Anchoring
techniques.definition: Establish a high reference point so the actual ask feels small.
techniques.example_skeleton: Most teams of your size end up at $120k/yr on this stack. We'd start you at $40k.

techniques.name: Specificity / credibility (precise numbers > round numbers)
techniques.definition: Use concrete, asymmetric numbers ("$47,310 saved" not "$50k saved") to signal real measurement.
techniques.when_to_avoid: When you actually don't have specific numbers — fabricated precision is worse than round honesty.
techniques.example_skeleton: Cut p99 from 412ms to 89ms in 11 days.
